title: Galak Fyyar
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|a military scientist from the Imperial Remnant|[[Galak]], a Naboo soldier}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Galak43.jpg]] |name=Galak Fyyar |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], ''[[Doomgiver]]''''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Blue |skin=Light |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] *[[Empire Reborn]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Ah, Captain Katarn. What a pleasure it is to finally meet you face to face.''"
"''The pleasure's all yours, I'm sure.|Galak and Kyle|audio=GalakmeetsKyle.flac|}} '''Galak Fyyar''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] who was a skilled commander, and a dedicated scientist that served [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Hethrir]]'s [[Empire Reborn]]. He, alongside [[Desann]] were tasked with the creation of [[Shadowtrooper]]s, [[The Force/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] empowered with [[artusian crystal]]s. He was later [[Death/Legends|killed]] [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Empire Reborn)|above]] [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. ==Biography== {{Quote|Within hours, Desann will have eradicated those annoying Jedi. Clearing the way for my ascent as the leader of the New Empire. I, Galak Fyyar the First, the genius who conceived of the Shadow Armor, will rule the galaxy with a cortosis fist! Worlds will tremble, stars will shudder—''"
"''Your shields will fall.|Galak Fyyar and Kyle Katarn|audio=Galakboast.flac|}} As an ambitious [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]], Fyyar rose to the rank of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] and had aspirations of galactic conquest, but his dependence on technology and his overconfidence were the keys to his eventual downfall. He also held the title of "Chancellor", though its significance is unclear. During his service in the Empire, Fyyar was famous not for his strategic or leadership skills, but rather for his technological innovations, working closely with Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].{{LucasArts|url=products/outcast/html/spin.html|text=N.R.I. Reports|int=N.R.I. Reports}} Before the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Fyyar worked at the [[Maw Installation]], but left to work in secret facilities around the galaxy just prior to the battle.{{LucasArts|url=products/outcast/html/characters_galak.html|text=Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast - Galak Fyyar}} After the death of the Emperor, Fyyar disappeared from the spotlight. He fell in with former [[Procurator of Justice]] [[Hethrir]] and his [[Empire Reborn]] faction. Fyyar became one of Hethrir's top lieutenants. He reappeared in the radars of [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] around [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], as he was consolidating [[Imperial Remnant]] force in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. He worked closely with [[Desann]], another of Hethrir's lieutenants, to develop methods of infusing non-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]] with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. The pair carried out their experiments on [[Kejim]] until the outpost was destroyed by [[Kyle Katarn]]. Desann and Fyyar managed to turn this to their advantage, tracking Katarn to the [[Valley of the Jedi]]. There, the pair put their research to use. [[Artusian crystal]]s focused the Valley's power into minimally Force-sensitive recruits, creating the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|dark Jedi]] order of [[Reborn]]. Fyyar also created a [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]]-based armor, resistant to [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]]. Fyyar himself had a special powered armor suit equipped with a rocket launcher, a [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generator]], and a [[Imperial Heavy Repeater|heavy repeater]]. Lighter cortosis armor, equipped with a focusing Artusian crystal, was issued to the [[shadowtrooper]]s, who served as the elite [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] troops of the Empire Reborn. [[File:Fyyar_armor.jpg|233x233px|thumb|left|Fyyar in his cortosis battle armor.]] With the success of their program, Desann and Fyyar initiated an assault on the [[Jedi Praxeum]] on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. Fyyar's ship, ''[[Doomgiver]]'', departed from his headquarters at the secret [[Cairn Installation]] and arrived over Yavin 4, where it deployed landing forces. Unknown to Fyyar, Katarn had infiltrated his operations. The [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] saboteur contacted [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], which attacked the ''Doomgiver'' upon its exit from hyperspace. Fyyar, in his battle suit, managed to surprise Katarn as he was in the process of destroying the ship's shield generator. Fyyar was too slow, however, and Katarn took out the generator with a lightsaber throw. Enraged, Fyyar attacked Katarn, but even his shielded cortosis armor proved no match for Katarn, who fought and struck the admiral down with his lightsaber. The suit malfunctioned and exploded, vaporizing Fyyar. ==Behind the scenes== Galak Fyyar was voiced by [[Steve Blum|Steven Jay Blum]]. During combat, Fyyar's shield generator can be disabled by destroying his armor's antenna with weapon fire. There are three ways to defeat Fyyar in his Cortosis armor, after the shield is brought down. The first one is to use force speed and your lightsaber combined to deliver extra fast strikes while his shield is recovering. Since force speed slows down the action and speeds you up, it will take a seemingly long time for his shield to regenerate, and you can deliver strikes much faster. The second way is to pummel him with force lightning. His armor seems to be highly vulnerable to that attack. This is also effective against his shield. The third way is to use saber throw on him. This will only work when the shield is down because if the player tries to hit the shield with saber throw, the saber will just go inside the shield, and the player will lose control of it on entry. The [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank plaque]] on Fyyar's uniform has a different layout than most others. Rather than consisting of two rows (the top row being four red bars and the bottom row being four blue bars), the plaque contains a single row of eight bars, alternating between red and blue bars every two bars. Furthermore, the plaque is conspicuously placed on the right side of the chest instead of the left side. There are another two red and blue bars worn on the collar which are larger than those worn on the chest. In the Multiplayer mode of ''Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy'' his name is written as "Galak Fyarr" if he is selected as a bot.''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' ==Appearances== *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]'' {{C|Multiplayer only}} {{Nc}} ==Sources== {{Mediacat|soundcat=Audio files of Galak Fyyar|imagecat=Images of Galak Fyyar}} *{{LucasArts|url=products/outcast/html/characters_galak.html|text=Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast - Galak Fyyar}} *{{LucasArts|url=products/outcast/html/spin.html|text=N.R.I. Reports|int=N.R.I. Reports}} {{1stm}} *{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050616a|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 6: Outcasts and Megalomaniacs Welcome}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/96/indexp2.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 1: Hierarchy of Power}} *''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{InsiderCite|152|The Rebel Bruiser: Steve Blum is Zeb Orrelios}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Galak Fyyar |es=Galak Fyyar |hu=Galak Fyyar |pl=Galak Fyyar |uk=Ґалак Фаяр }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Fyyar, Galak}} [[Category:Empire Reborn members]] [[Category:Galactic Empire science and engineering personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Imperial Navy admirals of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Males]]
